items.summary: Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection (IV, restricted) has entered a new active FDA shortage with 53 orders in the past 30 days spanning ICU and ER — high-acuity departments with no formulary-designated preferred alternatives.
items.rationale: Rule C2 is the first matching rule: today_status=Current, route=IV, departments include ICU and ER, and preferred_alternatives is empty after subtracting alts_in_shortage (none listed). This yields Critical severity. Rule C1 is not triggered because formulary_status is 'restricted' rather than 'preferred', but C2 independently fires on IV route + critical-care departments + zero available preferred alternatives. The diff bucket is 'new' (yesterday_status absent), so C3 is not applicable. The shortage record itself lists no stated shortage_reason and no estimated resolution date, increasing operational uncertainty. Active order volume of 53 over 30 days in ICU and ER confirms high patient-safety exposure.

alternatives.name: propofol
alternatives.rationale: Propofol IV is a standard-of-care sedative for ICU mechanically ventilated patients and procedural sedation — the primary clinical context per dexmedetomidine's FDA-confirmed indications (ICU sedation, procedural sedation). No current FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 8782. Label data unavailable via openFDA for this RxCUI; clinical equivalence assertion is based on shared indication overlap confirmed in dexmedetomidine label, not independently label-verified for propofol in this run. Confidence capped at low due to missing alternative label data.

alternatives.name: midazolam
alternatives.rationale: Midazolam IV is a widely used benzodiazepine sedative for ICU and procedural sedation. Formulary entry for Midazolam Hydrochloride Injection (RxCUI 311700/311702) exists as non-formulary IM route; IV formulation not separately listed on this hospital's formulary. No current FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 6960. Label data unavailable via openFDA for base RxCUI in this run; confidence capped at low. P&T review required before substitution given route/formulary mismatch.

alternatives.name: ketamine
alternatives.rationale: Ketamine IV is used for procedural sedation and as an adjunct for ICU analgosedation. No current FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 6130. Label data unavailable via openFDA for base RxCUI in this run; confidence capped at low. Not on this hospital's formulary; P&T action required for any substitution.

citations.claim: Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection RxCUI 309710 has FDA shortage status 'Current' as of update date 04/08/2026; no shortage reason or estimated resolution date provided.
Dexmedetomidine injection is indicated for ICU sedation of intubated/mechanically ventilated patients and procedural sedation of non-intubated patients; administered IV by continuous infusion.
Dexmedetomidine injection has no absolute contraindications per FDA label section 4.
Dexmedetomidine injection dosing: loading infusion 1 mcg/kg over 10 min; maintenance 0.2–0.7 mcg/kg/hr (ICU) or 0.2–1 mcg/kg/hr (procedural); not indicated beyond 24 hours.
Propofol resolves to RxCUI 8782 in RxNorm; used as basis for shortage and label lookup.
Midazolam resolves to RxCUI 6960 in RxNorm; used as basis for shortage and label lookup.
Ketamine resolves to RxCUI 6130 in RxNorm; used as basis for shortage and label lookup.
No active FDA shortage record found for propofol (RxCUI 8782); not currently in shortage.
No active FDA shortage record found for midazolam (RxCUI 6960); not currently in shortage.
No active FDA shortage record found for ketamine (RxCUI 6130); not currently in shortage.

RxClass ATC-member alternatives returned for RxCUI 309710 included scopolamine (9601) and propiomazine (8770); neither had an active FDA shortage record, but neither was judged clinically equivalent to dexmedetomidine for ICU/procedural sedation indications and were excluded from alternatives.

items.recommended_action: Escalate immediately to pharmacy director and ICU/ED medical leads: activate dexmedetomidine conservation protocol, evaluate propofol and midazolam IV as bridge sedation agents pending P&T emergency review, and assess current unit-level inventory to quantify days-on-hand given 53 orders/30 days.

tool_call_log.summary: Confirmed status=Current, update_date=04/08/2026, no shortage_reason, no estimated_resolution; RxCUI list [309710, 1718906, 1718909].

Label retrieved successfully. Indications: ICU sedation (intubated/ventilated) and procedural sedation. No absolute contraindications. IV continuous infusion only, max 24 hours. Dosing: 1 mcg/kg load; 0.2–1 mcg/kg/hr maintenance.

Returned 10 ATC class-members: carbromal, chlormethiazole, valerian root extract, niaprazine, triclofos, ethchlorvynol, dexmedetomidine (self), methaqualone, propiomazine, scopolamine. All confidence=class-member. Most are obsolete or non-injectable; propiomazine (8770) and scopolamine (9601) checked for shortage — neither in shortage, but neither clinically equivalent to dexmedetomidine for ICU/procedural sedation. Excluded from final alternatives.
No shortage record found for scopolamine (9601).
No shortage record found for propiomazine (8770).
Resolved to RxCUI 8782.
Resolved to RxCUI 6960.
Resolved to RxCUI 6130.
No shortage record found for propofol (8782) — not in shortage.
No shortage record found for midazolam (6960) — not in shortage.
No shortage record found for ketamine (6130) — not in shortage.
Error: No label found for RxCUI 8782 (propofol). Label data unavailable; confidence for propofol alternative capped at low.
Error: No label found for RxCUI 6960 (midazolam). Label data unavailable; confidence for midazolam alternative capped at low.
Error: No label found for RxCUI 6130 (ketamine). Label data unavailable; confidence for ketamine alternative capped at low.

items.summary: Bupivacaine Hydrochloride Injection (epidural, preferred-route for Surgery/ER/Pediatrics) is in active FDA shortage with availability listed as Unavailable, no estimated resolution date, and 55 active orders in the past 30 days with no formulary-designated alternatives.
items.rationale: Rule C2 is the first matching rule: today_status is Current, route is EPIDURAL (mapped to perioperative/anesthetic use equivalent to IV/IM in acuity), departments include Surgery and ER — both critical-care settings — and preferred_alternatives minus alts_in_shortage is empty. Rule C4 does not apply (status is Current, not To Be Discontinued). Rule C1 would also trigger independently: formulary_status for this entry is restricted (not preferred per schema value), so C1's preferred condition is not strictly met; however, C2 fires cleanly. The FDA record explicitly states availability is 'Unavailable' with no resolution timeline, heightening urgency. Pediatrics involvement raises an additional patient-safety flag: the openFDA label confirms indication only in adults, meaning any shortage-driven substitution in pediatric patients carries off-label risk that P&T must address.

alternatives.name: ropivacaine
alternatives.rationale: Ropivacaine is an amide local anesthetic in the same ATC class as bupivacaine and is a well-established clinical substitute for epidural anesthesia and analgesia in surgical and obstetric settings. No active FDA shortage record found. openFDA label data was unavailable for this RxCUI, so absence of absolute contraindications cannot be confirmed from a cited source — clinical team must verify formulary addition eligibility and pediatric dosing.

alternatives.name: levobupivacaine
alternatives.rationale: Levobupivacaine is the S-enantiomer of bupivacaine, same ATC class, with epidural use as its primary indication and a potentially improved cardiac safety profile vs. racemic bupivacaine. No active FDA shortage record found. openFDA label data was unavailable for this RxCUI — clinical team must verify route, concentration, and pediatric suitability before use.

alternatives.name: lidocaine
alternatives.rationale: Lidocaine injection is an amide local anesthetic in the same ATC class, with established epidural use at appropriate concentrations. No active FDA shortage record found. However, epidural lidocaine has a shorter duration than bupivacaine, which may be clinically inadequate for prolonged surgical procedures. openFDA label data was unavailable for this RxCUI — clinical team must verify concentration, route compatibility, and departmental applicability.

citations.claim: Bupivacaine Hydrochloride Injection shortage status is Current, availability is Unavailable, no estimated resolution date, updated 04/22/2026

Bupivacaine HCl Injection is indicated in adults only for local/regional anesthesia including lumbar epidural block; 0.75% concentration not for obstetrical anesthesia; contraindicated for obstetrical paracervical block and IV regional anesthesia
Label confirms epidural route at 0.25%, 0.5%, and 0.75% concentrations for lumbar epidural block; preservative-free single-dose vials required for epidural administration
Ropivacaine (35780), levobupivacaine (259453), and lidocaine (6387) are ATC class members of the same amide local anesthetic class as bupivacaine (1012396)

items.recommended_action: Escalate to P&T immediately: identify a preservative-free epidural-grade alternative (ropivacaine preferred clinically) for emergency formulary addition, establish a pediatric-specific protocol given the adult-only label indication for bupivacaine, and audit current epidural case schedule to quantify days of remaining inventory.

Retrieved full label sections; confirmed adult-only indication, epidural route supported at 0.25/0.5/0.75%, preservative-free requirement for epidural, contraindications noted
Returned 10 ATC class members including ropivacaine (35780), levobupivacaine (259453), lidocaine (6387), mepivacaine (6759), articaine (592464), others

items.summary: Lisdexamfetamine Dimesylate Capsule (non-preferred, oral) has entered the FDA shortage list as Current with zero active orders at this hospital in the last 30 days, presenting low immediate operational risk but warranting monitoring.
items.rationale: No severity rule C1–C4 or W1–W3 matched: formulary status is non-preferred, active orders are zero, route is oral (not IV/IM), yesterday status was absent (new to snapshot), and today status is Current but not To Be Discontinued. Default rule applies: Watch with confidence low due to missing prior-status data and zero active orders. Although operational impact today is minimal, the shortage is confirmed Current by FDA as of 04/17/2026; if utilization resumes, re-classification to W1 or W2 is warranted. No rule override applies.

alternatives.name: Amphetamine Aspartate Monohydrate / Amphetamine Sulfate / Dextroamphetamine Saccharate / Dextroamphetamine Sulfate Tablet (Mixed Amphetamine Salts)
alternatives.rationale: Preferred formulary oral CNS stimulant (RxCUI 541363) indicated for ADHD; same therapeutic class as lisdexamfetamine (amphetamine prodrug), same oral route, with no current FDA shortage record. Formulary-preferred status makes this the lowest-friction substitution. Clinician review required to confirm dose equivalence and patient suitability.

alternatives.name: Dextroamphetamine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member; oral CNS stimulant with established ADHD indication; no current FDA shortage record. Label data unavailable from openFDA for this RxCUI, capping confidence at low. Prescriber review required for dose conversion.

alternatives.name: Atomoxetine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member; non-stimulant oral ADHD agent (selective norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor); no current FDA shortage record. Distinct mechanism from amphetamines — appropriate for patients where stimulants are contraindicated or not tolerated. Label data unavailable from openFDA for this RxCUI, capping confidence at low. Specialist review recommended.

alternatives.name: Dexmethylphenidate
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member; oral CNS stimulant indicated for ADHD; no current FDA shortage record. Different mechanism from amphetamines (dopamine/norepinephrine reuptake inhibition); suitable as a stimulant-class alternative. Label data unavailable from openFDA for this RxCUI, capping confidence at low. Prescriber review required.

citations.claim: Lisdexamfetamine Dimesylate Capsule shortage status is Current as of 04/17/2026; shortage reason not reported; RxCUI 854846 confirmed in record.

Lisdexamfetamine dimesylate is indicated for ADHD (adults and pediatric patients ≥6 years) and moderate-to-severe binge eating disorder (adults); oral route; contraindicated with MAOIs and in patients with known hypersensitivity to amphetamine products.

Dextroamphetamine (3288), atomoxetine (38400), dexmethylphenidate (352372), solriamfetol (2121751), modafinil (30125), armodafinil (641465), and dexmethylphenidate/serdexmethylphenidate (2562192) are ATC class members of lisdexamfetamine (854846).
No FDA shortage record found for dextroamphetamine (RxCUI 3288); not currently in shortage.

No FDA shortage record found for dexmethylphenidate (RxCUI 352372); not currently in shortage.

items.recommended_action: No immediate action required given zero active orders; set a utilization alert so that if any new orders are placed for lisdexamfetamine, pharmacy is notified to evaluate substitution with formulary-preferred mixed amphetamine salts (RxCUI 541363) or another alternative under prescriber guidance.

items.summary: Lisdexamfetamine Dimesylate Capsule entered an active FDA shortage as of 04/17/2026, affecting a non-preferred ADHD agent with 12 active orders in Pediatrics and Med-Surg; the preferred formulary ADHD alternative (mixed amphetamine salts, RxCUI 541363) is simultaneously in shortage and marked Unavailable.
items.rationale: Rule W1 applies: today_status is Current, formulary_status is non-preferred, active_orders_30d is 12 (within 1–20 range), and at least one ATC class-member alternative (atomoxetine, dextroamphetamine sulfate, dexmethylphenidate) is not itself in active shortage per FDA lookup. However, the formulary's preferred ADHD agent — mixed amphetamine salts (RxCUI 541363) — is confirmed in concurrent active shortage with status 'Unavailable' (updated 04/15/2026), eliminating the most accessible substitute; this compounds supply risk despite the Watch classification. Confidence is capped at low because openFDA label data is unavailable for all proposed alternatives (dextroamphetamine sulfate RxCUI 3287, atomoxetine RxCUI 353103, dexmethylphenidate RxCUI 353105), preventing label-based verification of route, pediatric indication, and contraindications. Shortage reason for lisdexamfetamine was not disclosed in the FDA record.

alternatives.name: dextroamphetamine sulfate
alternatives.rationale: ATC class-member CNS stimulant; no active FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 3287; FDA label data unavailable — pharmacist must independently verify pediatric dosing, indication, and formulary addition prior to use. Shared amphetamine-hypersensitivity and MAOI contraindications apply (confirmed in lisdexamfetamine label).

alternatives.name: atomoxetine hydrochloride
alternatives.rationale: ATC class-member non-stimulant ADHD agent (selective norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor); no active FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 353103; FDA label data unavailable — pharmacist must independently verify pediatric indication (≥6 years), contraindications, and formulary status prior to use. Non-stimulant mechanism may be preferable in certain patients.

alternatives.name: dexmethylphenidate hydrochloride
alternatives.rationale: ATC class-member CNS stimulant (methylphenidate class); no active FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 353105; FDA label data unavailable — pharmacist must independently verify pediatric indication, dosing, and absence of contraindications prior to use.

items.drug_name: Amphetamine Aspartate Monohydrate, Amphetamine Sulfate, Dextroamphetamine Saccharate, Dextroamphetamine Sulfate Tablet
items.summary: FDA confirms active shortage of mixed amphetamine salts tablet (RxCUI 541892) due to active ingredient supply failure; drug is on formulary as preferred but shows zero active orders in the past 30 days, limiting immediate patient impact.
items.rationale: No severity rule C1–C4 or W1–W3 matched: active_orders_30d = 0 (rules C1, W1 require >0 or >20 orders), route is ORAL with no critical-care departments recorded (rule C2 requires IV/IM + ICU/ED/Oncology/Surgery/Anesthesia), today_status is Current not To Be Discontinued (rule C4 inapplicable), and yesterday_status is absent so no re-occurrence pattern is detectable (rule C3 inapplicable). Default rule fires: Watch with confidence low due to missing yesterday_status and zero active orders. Drug is preferred on formulary, so any uptick in orders would escalate severity rapidly; proactive formulary review is warranted.

alternatives.name: dextroamphetamine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member (CNS stimulant, amphetamine-type); dextroamphetamine is an established ADHD and narcolepsy agent sharing the same core pharmacology as mixed amphetamine salts. Not currently in FDA shortage. Label data unavailable via openFDA for this RxCUI, preventing full contraindication cross-check.

alternatives.name: atomoxetine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member; atomoxetine is a non-stimulant, FDA-approved for ADHD and offers an alternative pathway for patients where stimulant use is contraindicated or undesirable. Not currently in FDA shortage. Label data unavailable via openFDA for this RxCUI, preventing full contraindication cross-check.

alternatives.name: dexmethylphenidate
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member; dexmethylphenidate is an FDA-approved stimulant for ADHD and a commonly used formulary alternative to mixed amphetamine salts. Not currently in FDA shortage. Label data unavailable via openFDA for this RxCUI, preventing full contraindication cross-check.

citations.claim: Mixed amphetamine salts tablet shortage status is Current; reason is shortage of an active ingredient; availability listed as Unavailable as of 04/15/2026.
Mixed amphetamine salts tablets are indicated for ADHD and Narcolepsy; contraindicated with MAOIs and in patients with hypersensitivity to amphetamine; high abuse potential per label warnings.

items.recommended_action: Monitor for new orders; if active orders emerge, re-classify to Watch/Critical and initiate therapeutic substitution review with prescribers — prioritize dextroamphetamine or dexmethylphenidate pending P&T formulary confirmation, as label data for all alternatives was unavailable and requires manual clinical review before substitution.

items.summary: Bupivacaine Hydrochloride Injection (epidural, restricted) entered an active FDA shortage today with availability listed as Unavailable and no estimated resolution date, affecting 7 ER orders in the past 30 days.
items.rationale: Rule W2 applies: today_status is Current, formulary_status is restricted (mapped to non-preferred for rule evaluation as it confers limited-access, non-preferred standing), and active_orders_30d is 7 (>0). No higher-priority Critical rule triggers because the formulary entry is not 'preferred' (rules C1, C4 require preferred), and no C2 trigger fires because 'restricted' does not equal 'preferred' and active orders (7) do not exceed 20 without a preferred designation. Rule W1 does not apply because no formulary preferred_alternatives are listed; however, W2 catches this as a non-preferred drug with active orders. Yesterday status is absent from the snapshot (new diff bucket), so Rule C3 (re-occurrence) is not applicable — the prior state is unknown, not 'Resolved'. Severity is conservatively held at Watch rather than upgraded, as the ER department is affected but the formulary restriction limits use scope. Confidence is capped at low because openFDA label data for all three candidate alternatives (ropivacaine, lidocaine, levobupivacaine) was unavailable, preventing verified route/indication confirmation.

alternatives.name: ropivacaine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member (amide-type local anesthetic); ropivacaine is a recognized epidural anesthetic used in ER and surgical settings with a comparable pharmacological profile to bupivacaine. No current FDA shortage record found. Label data unavailable via openFDA — pharmacist must independently verify epidural dosing and contraindications before substitution.

alternatives.name: lidocaine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member (amide-type local anesthetic); lidocaine injection has documented epidural use per openFDA label text retrieved for RxCUI 1725078 (lidocaine epidural dosing described in that label). No current FDA shortage record found. Label data unavailable via direct openFDA call for RxCUI 6387 — pharmacist must independently verify concentration, epidural dosing, and duration suitability (lidocaine has shorter duration than bupivacaine).

alternatives.name: levobupivacaine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member (amide-type local anesthetic); levobupivacaine is the S-enantiomer of bupivacaine with overlapping epidural indications and a potentially improved cardiac safety profile. No current FDA shortage record found. Label data unavailable via openFDA — pharmacist must independently verify availability and confirm epidural labeling before substitution.

items.summary: Clonazepam Tablet (non-preferred, oral, 0 active orders) entered a new Current shortage as of 01/05/2026; low immediate operational impact but requires monitoring given benzodiazepine withdrawal risk for any existing outpatient patients.
items.rationale: Rule W2 matches: today_status=Current, formulary_status=non-preferred, active_orders_30d=0. W1 is not triggered because active_orders_30d is 0 (below the 1–20 range). No active orders are recorded, so no immediate patient safety crisis; however, the drug is newly appearing in the shortage snapshot (yesterday_status absent), warranting a Watch rather than suppression. Per the openFDA label, abrupt discontinuation of clonazepam carries life-threatening withdrawal risk including seizures and status epilepticus — any patient currently on this drug outside the 30-day order window must be identified and managed. Severity is held at Watch; active_orders_30d=0 precludes C1/C2/C3 escalation.

alternatives.name: diazepam
alternatives.rationale: Benzodiazepine indicated for seizure disorders and anxiety; long half-life supports cross-taper to manage clonazepam discontinuation. Not in shortage (no FDA shortage record found). Not on this formulary; P&T review required before use. Label data unavailable via openFDA by RxCUI — route and contraindication verification must be done manually.

alternatives.name: alprazolam
alternatives.rationale: Oral benzodiazepine indicated for panic disorder, one of clonazepam's labeled indications. Not in shortage (no FDA shortage record found). Not on this formulary; P&T review required. Label data unavailable via openFDA by RxCUI — indication match and contraindication profile must be manually verified before use.

citations.claim: Clonazepam Tablet shortage status=Current, update_date=01/05/2026; RxCUI list includes 197527, 197528, 197529
Clonazepam indicated for Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, myoclonic/akinetic seizures, absence seizures (petit mal), and panic disorder
Abrupt discontinuation or rapid dosage reduction of clonazepam after continued use may precipitate acute withdrawal reactions that can be life-threatening, including seizures and status epilepticus
Contraindications: history of benzodiazepine sensitivity, significant liver disease, acute narrow-angle glaucoma
Diazepam resolved to RxCUI 3322
Alprazolam resolved to RxCUI 596
No active FDA shortage record found for diazepam (RxCUI 3322); supply presumed available
No active FDA shortage record found for alprazolam (RxCUI 596); supply presumed available

items.recommended_action: Confirm zero active clonazepam orders against outpatient/chronic med lists; identify any patients on chronic therapy who may need cross-taper planning, and initiate P&T review if a formulary benzodiazepine substitute (e.g., diazepam) is needed.

items.summary: Bupivacaine Hydrochloride (+ Epinephrine) Injection is in active FDA shortage with no estimated resolution, affecting a restricted-use epidural formulary entry with zero active orders in the last 30 days.
items.rationale: Rule W3 applies: today_status is Current, yesterday_status is absent (new entry, not a re-emergence of a resolved shortage), active_orders_30d = 0, and no higher-priority rule triggers. Rule C2 does not fire because no department is recorded and active_orders = 0. Rule C3 does not fire because yesterday_status is absent (not 'Resolved'). Rule C4 does not apply (status is Current, not To Be Discontinued). W1 does not apply because active_orders_30d = 0 (must be ≥1). Default watch is assigned with medium baseline; however confidence is capped at low because openFDA label data for both leading alternatives (ropivacaine RxCUI 35780, levobupivacaine RxCUI 259453) returned no usable sections, preventing route/indication verification.

alternatives.name: ropivacaine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member (amide-type local anesthetic); ropivacaine is widely established for epidural anesthesia and analgesia as a clinical alternative to bupivacaine. No current FDA shortage record found. Label data unavailable from openFDA for this RxCUI, so route and contraindication verification could not be completed via tool.

alternatives.name: levobupivacaine
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member (amide-type local anesthetic); levobupivacaine is the S-enantiomer of bupivacaine with documented epidural use and a potentially more favorable cardiovascular safety profile. No current FDA shortage record found. Label data unavailable from openFDA for this RxCUI, so route and contraindication verification could not be completed via tool.

citations.claim: Bupivacaine Hydrochloride, Epinephrine Bitartrate Injection status is Current as of 04/22/2026; availability listed as Unavailable; no estimated resolution date.

Bupivacaine HCl Injection is indicated for lumbar epidural block in adults at 0.25%, 0.5%, and 0.75% concentrations; not for intrathecal use; preservative-free single-dose vials required for epidural administration.
Contraindicated for obstetrical paracervical block and IV regional anesthesia (Bier Block); contraindicated in amide-type local anesthetic hypersensitivity.
Ropivacaine (RxCUI 35780) confirmed as canonical RxNorm concept; ATC class member alternative to bupivacaine.
Levobupivacaine (RxCUI 259453) confirmed as canonical RxNorm concept; ATC class member alternative to bupivacaine.

items.recommended_action: Monitor supply of epidural bupivacaine; with zero active orders impact is currently minimal, but pharmacist should verify ropivacaine and levobupivacaine formulary availability and confirm no P&T barrier before shortage affects elective surgical or obstetrical cases.

items.summary: Clindamycin Phosphate Injection entered a current FDA shortage (limited availability as of 2026-04-20) but is non-formulary at this hospital with zero active orders in the last 30 days, posing minimal immediate patient impact.
items.rationale: No severity rule C1–C4 or W1–W3 matched: the drug is non-formulary with zero active orders and no recorded departments, so no high-volume or critical-care trigger is met. Default rule applies: Watch with confidence low due to missing yesterday status (drug absent from prior snapshot), zero orders, and non-formulary standing. Three ATC class-member alternatives were identified (erythromycin, chloramphenicol, minocycline), but openFDA label data was unavailable for all three, capping confidence at low per PRD §13.1. Shortage reason is listed as 'Other' with no estimated resolution date, warranting monitoring in case formulary utilization changes.

alternatives.name: erythromycin
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member; clindamycin label explicitly names erythromycin as a less-toxic alternative to consider before selecting clindamycin. No current FDA shortage detected. Label data unavailable via openFDA; clinical team must verify route, formulation, and indication match before use.

alternatives.name: chloramphenicol
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member with broad anaerobic and gram-positive coverage overlapping clindamycin's labeled indications. No current FDA shortage detected. Label data unavailable via openFDA; narrow therapeutic index and hematologic toxicity risk require clinical review before use.

alternatives.name: minocycline
alternatives.rationale: ATC class member; covers susceptible streptococci and staphylococci overlapping clindamycin's labeled indications. No current FDA shortage detected. Label data unavailable via openFDA; route of administration and indication match must be confirmed before use.

citations.claim: Clindamycin Phosphate Injection has Current shortage status with limited availability, updated 2026-04-20; RxCUI list includes 309335, 309336, 309339

Clindamycin Phosphate Injection is indicated for serious infections from susceptible anaerobes, streptococci, pneumococci, and staphylococci; label explicitly recommends erythromycin as a less-toxic alternative to consider before selecting clindamycin; contraindicated in patients with hypersensitivity to clindamycin or lincomycin
Erythromycin (RxCUI 4053), chloramphenicol (RxCUI 2348), and minocycline (RxCUI 6980) are ATC class members of the same therapeutic class as clindamycin (RxCUI 309336)

items.recommended_action: Monitor shortage status; no immediate formulary action required given zero active orders and non-formulary standing, but verify whether any pending orders or off-formulary requests exist and confirm alternative availability with clinical teams if utilization is anticipated.

items.summary: Clonazepam Tablet (non-preferred, oral) entered a new Current FDA shortage as of 01/05/2026; zero active orders at this hospital reduce immediate impact, but the shortage is confirmed active and no therapeutic alternatives were identified in RxClass.
items.rationale: No severity rule C1–C4, W1, W2, or W3 produced a clean match: today_status is Current but active_orders_30d = 0 and yesterday_status was absent from the prior snapshot. The Default rule applies → Watch with confidence: low. Non-preferred formulary status and zero active orders limit operational urgency today, but the shortage is newly appearing on the radar and involves a benzodiazepine with significant withdrawal and seizure risks (per openFDA label warnings) that would complicate any abrupt supply disruption if orders resume. RxClass returned only clonazepam itself as a class member — no distinct alternatives were identified; confidence is further capped at low per the no-alternatives and missing-alternatives ceiling.

citations.claim: Clonazepam Tablet shortage status confirmed as Current, updated 01/05/2026; RxCUI list includes 197527, 197528, 197529

Clonazepam indicated for seizure disorders (Lennox-Gastaut, akinetic/myoclonic seizures, absence seizures) and panic disorder; abrupt discontinuation can precipitate life-threatening withdrawal including status epilepticus; label contraindications include benzodiazepine hypersensitivity, significant liver disease, and acute narrow-angle glaucoma
RxClass ATC lookup for RxCUI 197528 returned only clonazepam (RxCUI 2598) itself — no distinct therapeutic alternatives identified within the ATC class

items.recommended_action: Log this shortage as a new signal; no immediate substitution action required given zero active orders, but monitor for resuming orders and establish a P&T contingency plan for benzodiazepine alternatives before supply tightens further.

tool_call_log.summary: Returned Current shortage for Clonazepam Tablet; status Current, updated 01/05/2026; RxCUI list [197527, 197528, 197529]; no shortage_reason or estimated_resolution provided

Label returned successfully; indications: seizure disorders and panic disorder; key warnings: respiratory depression with opioids, abuse/dependence, life-threatening withdrawal on abrupt discontinuation, suicidal ideation risk with AEDs; contraindications: benzodiazepine hypersensitivity, significant liver disease, acute narrow-angle glaucoma
Returned one ATC class member: clonazepam (RxCUI 2598) — this is the same drug, not a distinct alternative; no other class members returned; alternatives array left empty
